sections.label: Section 981b
sections.translation: And where must I go from there ?

sections.label: Section 982
sections.translation: To the land of Thesprotia .

sections.label: Section 982b
sections.translation: To Dodona ’ s holy threshold ?

sections.label: Section 983
sections.translation: You understand .

sections.label: Section 983b
sections.original: τί δὴ τόδʼ ἔρυμά μοι γενήσεται ;
sections.translation: What protection will I find there ?

sections.label: Section 984
sections.translation: The god will send you on your way .

sections.label: Section 984b
sections.translation: How shall I find the means ?

sections.label: Section 985
sections.translation: I will supply you with money .

sections.label: Section 985b
sections.translation: A good plan of yours , father . Go now ; for I will come to your sister , Jocasta , at whose breast I was suckled when bereft of my mother , a lonely orphan , to give her greeting and then I will save my life .

sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἶα , χώρει · μὴ τὸ σὸν κωλυέτω . —
sections.translation: Come , come ! be going ; it isn ’ t your part to hinder me . Exit Creon .

sections.label: Section 991
sections.original: γυναῖκες , ὡς εὖ πατρὸς ἐξεῖλον φόβον ,
sections.translation: How cleverly , ladies , I banished my father ’ s fears by crafty words to gain my end ; for he is trying to get me away , depriving the city of its chance and surrendering me to cowardice . Though an old man may be pardoned ,

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: γέροντι , τοὐμὸν δʼ οὐχὶ συγγνώμην ἔχει ,
sections.translation: yet in my case there is no pardon for betraying the country that gave me birth . Know this , I will go and save the city , and give my life up for this land . For it is shameful : those whom no oracles bind

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.translation: and who have not come under divine necessity , stand there , shoulder to shoulder , with no fear of death , and fight for their country before her towers ; while I leave the land like a coward , a traitor to my father and brother and city ;

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.original: ἄπειμʼ · ὅπου δʼ ἂν ζῶ , κακὸς φανήσομαι .
sections.translation: wherever I live , I shall seem base .

sections.label: Section 1006
sections.original: μὰ τὸν μετʼ ἄστρων Ζῆνʼ Ἄρη τε φοίνιον ,
sections.translation: No , by Zeus and all his stars , by Ares , god of blood , who established the Sown-men that sprung one day from earth as lords of this land ! I will go , and standing on the topmost battlements ,

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.translation: will sacrifice myself over the dragon ’ s deep , dark den , the spot the seer described , and will set my country free . I have spoken . Now I go to make the city a present of my life , no mean offering , to rid this kingdom of its affliction .

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: εἰ γὰρ λαβὼν ἕκαστος ὅ τι δύναιτό τις
sections.translation: For if each were to take and expend all the good within his power , contributing it to the common good of his country , our states would experience fewer troubles and would prosper for the future . Exit Menoeceus .

sections.label: Section 1.1019
sections.translation: You came , you came , O winged creature , born of earth

sections.label: Section 1.1020
sections.translation: and hellish viper , to prey upon the sons of Cadmus , full of death , full of sorrow , half a maiden , a murderous monster , with roving wings

sections.label: Section 1.1025
sections.translation: and ravening claws ; you once caught up youths from the haunts of Dirce , with discordant song ,

sections.label: Section 1.1030
sections.translation: and you brought , you brought a murderous grief , a deadly curse to our native land . A deadly god he was who brought all this to pass . Mourning of mothers , mourning of maidens ,

sections.label: Section 1.1035
sections.translation: filled the houses with groans ; a lamenting cry , a lamenting song , one after another wailed out , in turn throughout the city . The roar of the groaning

sections.label: Section 1.1040
sections.translation: was like thunder , whenever the winged maiden bore a man out of sight from the city .

sections.label: Section 1.1043
sections.translation: At last came Oedipus , the man of sorrow , sent from Delphi

sections.label: Section 1.1045
sections.translation: to this land of Thebes , a joy to us then , but afterwards a cause of grief ; for , when he guessed the riddle triumphantly , he formed with his mother an unhallowed union , woe to him !

sections.label: Section 1.1050
sections.translation: polluting the city ; and striking down his sons by his curses , he handed them over to loathsome strife , through blood , the wretched man .

sections.label: Section 1.1054
sections.translation: We admire him , we admire him ,

sections.label: Section 1.1055
sections.translation: who has gone to his death in his country ’ s cause , leaving tears to Creon , but bringing a crown of victory to our seven fenced towers .

sections.label: Section 1.1060
sections.translation: May we be mothers in this way , may we have such fair children , dear PalIas , you who with well-aimed stone spilled the serpent ’ s blood , rousing Cadmus to brood upon the task ,

sections.label: Section 1.1065
sections.translation: from which a demon ’ s curse swooped upon this land and ravaged it .

sections.label: Section 1067
sections.original: ὠή , τίς ἐν πύλαισι δωμάτων κυρεῖ ;
sections.translation: Ho there ! Who is at the palace-gates ? Open the door , summon Jocasta forth . Ho there ! once again I call ; in spite of this long delay ,

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.translation: come forth ; listen , noble wife of Oedipus , cease your lamentation and your tears of woe .

sections.label: Section 1072
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ , οὔ που ξυμφορὰν ἥκεις φέρων
sections.translation: Surely you have not come , dear friend , with the sad news of Eteocles ’ death , beside whose shield you have always marched , warding off from him the enemy ’ s darts ?

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original: τί μοί ποθʼ ἥκεις καινὸν ἀγγελῶν ἔπος ;
sections.translation: What tidings are you here to bring me ? Is my son alive or dead ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 1077
sections.original: ζῇ , μὴ τρέσῃς τόδʼ , ὥς σʼ ἀπαλλάξω φόβου .
sections.translation: He is alive , do not fear that , so that I may rid you of your terror .

sections.label: Section 1078
sections.original: τί δʼ ; ἑπτάπυργοι πῶς ἔχουσι περιβολαί ;
sections.translation: Well ? How is it with the seven towers that wall us in ?

sections.label: Section 1079
sections.translation: They stand unshattered ; the city is not plundered .

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: ἦλθον δὲ πρὸς κίνδυνον Ἀργείου δορός ;
sections.translation: Have they been in jeopardy of the Argive spear ?

sections.label: Section 1081
sections.original: ἀκμήν γʼ ἐπʼ αὐτήν · ἀλλʼ ὁ Καδμείων Ἄρης
sections.translation: Yes , on the very brink ; but our Theban warriors proved stronger than Mycenae ’ s might .

sections.label: Section 1083
sections.original: ἓν εἰπὲ πρὸς θεῶν , εἴ τι Πολυνείκους πέρι
sections.translation: One thing tell me , by the gods , if you know anything of Polyneices ; for this too is my concern , if he is alive .

sections.label: Section 1085
sections.original: ζῇ σοι ξυνωρὶς ἐς τόδʼ ἡμέρας τέκνων .
sections.translation: As yet your sons are living , the pair of them .

sections.label: Section 1086
sections.translation: God bless you ! How did you succeed in beating off from our gates the Argive army , when beleaguered ? Tell me , so that I may go within and cheer the old blind man , since our city is still safe .

sections.label: Section 1090
sections.original: ἐπεὶ Κρέοντος παῖς ὁ γῆς ὑπερθανὼν
sections.translation: After Creon ’ s son , who gave up his life for his country , had taken his stand on the turret ’ s top and plunged a dark-hilted sword through his throat to save this land , your son told off seven companies with their captains to the seven gates to keep watch on the Argive warriors ,

sections.label: Section 1095
sections.original: σὸς παῖς , ἐφέδρους δʼ ἱππότας μὲν ἱππόταις
sections.translation: and stationed cavalry to cover cavalry , and infantry to support infantry , so that assistance might be close at hand for any weak point in the walls . Then from our lofty towers we saw the Argive army with their white shields leaving

sections.label: Section 1100
sections.translation: Teumesus , and , when near the trench , they charged up to our Theban city at a run . In one loud burst from their ranks and from our walls rang out the battle-cry and trumpet-call .

sections.label: Section 1104
sections.original: καὶ πρῶτα μὲν προσῆγε Νηίταις πύλαις
sections.translation: First to the Neitian gate , Parthenopaeus , son of the huntress ,

sections.label: Section 1105
sections.translation: led a company bristling with thick rows of shields , and he had his own device in the centre of his shield : Atalanta slaying the Aetolian boar with an arrow shot from far . To the gates of Proetus

sections.label: Section 1110
sections.original: πύλας ἐχώρει σφάγιʼ ἔχων ἐφʼ ἅρματι
sections.translation: came the prophet Amphiaraus , bringing the victims on a chariot ; he had no boastful sign , but weapons chastely plain .

sections.label: Section 1113
sections.original: Ὠγύγια δʼ ἐς πυλώμαθʼ Ἱππομέδων ἄναξ
sections.translation: Next lord Hippomedon came marching to the Ogygian gates with this device in the middle of his shield :

sections.label: Section 1115
sections.translation: Argus the all-seeing dappled with eyes on the watch , some open with the rising stars , others hiding when they set , as could be seen after he was slain .

sections.label: Section 1119
sections.original: Ὁμολωίσιν δὲ τάξιν εἶχε πρὸς πύλαις
sections.translation: At the Homoloian gates Tydeus had his post ,

sections.label: Section 1120
sections.original: Τυδεύς , λέοντος δέρος ἔχων ἐπʼ ἀσπίδι
sections.translation: a lion ’ s skin with shaggy mane upon his shield , while the Titan Prometheus bore a torch in his right hand , to fire the town .

sections.label: Section 1123
sections.original: ὁ σὸς δὲ Κρηναίαισι Πολυνείκης πύλαις
sections.translation: Your own Polyneices led the battle against the Fountain gate ; upon his shield for a device

sections.label: Section 1125
sections.translation: were the colts of Potniae galloping at frantic speed , revolving by some clever contrivance on pivots by the handle , so as to appear distraught .

sections.label: Section 1128
sections.original: ὁ δʼ οὐκ ἔλασσον Ἄρεος ἐς μάχην φρονῶν
sections.translation: At Electra ’ s gate Capaneus brought up his company , bold as Ares for the battle ;

sections.label: Section 1130
sections.translation: this device his shield bore upon its iron back : an earth-born giant carrying on his shoulders a whole city which he had wrenched from its base , a hint to us of the fate in store for Thebes .

sections.label: Section 1134
sections.original: ταῖς δʼ ἑβδόμαις Ἄδραστος ἐν πύλαισιν ἦν ,
sections.translation: Adrastus was at the seventh gate ;

sections.label: Section 1135
sections.translation: a hundred vipers engraved on his shield , as he bore on his left arm the hydra the boast of Argos , and serpents were carrying off in their jaws the sons of Thebes from within our very walls . Now I was able to see each of them ,

sections.label: Section 1140
sections.translation: as I carried the watch-word along to the leaders of our companies .

sections.label: Section 1141
sections.original: καὶ πρῶτα μὲν τόξοισι καὶ μεσαγκύλοις
sections.translation: To begin with , we fought with bows and thonged javelins , with slings that shoot from far and crashing stones ; and as we were conquering , Tydeus and your son suddenly cried aloud :
